---
title: "Notes"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

<div class="landing-stack">

::: {.landing-hero}
<span class="hero-eyebrow">VfM source notes</span>

# Start with the source, then map the concepts

This section collects value for money evaluation notes. Start with the definitions synthesis, then follow the source notes and map to see how citations and linked source material connect.

<div class="page-metadata">
  <span>Source notes</span>
  <span>VfM criteria</span>
  <span>Evidence map</span>
  <span><a href="../references.bib" download>Export BibTeX</a></span>
</div>
:::

## Source Notes {#source-notes .section-heading}

```{=html}
<div class="notes-filter" role="search" aria-label="Filter source note tiles">
  <label for="notes-search">Find
    <input id="notes-search" type="search" placeholder="Search notes" autocomplete="off" />
  </label>
  <label for="notes-section-filter">Section
    <select id="notes-section-filter">
      <option value="all">All sections</option>
      <option value="Overview">Overview</option>
      <option value="Central Gov Guidance">Central Gov Guidance</option>
      <option value="King and VfI">King and VfI</option>
      <option value="OECD DAC">OECD DAC</option>
      <option value="DfT">DfT</option>
      <option value="Sourcing Playbook">Sourcing Playbook</option>
    </select>
  </label>
  <label for="notes-type-filter">Type
    <select id="notes-type-filter">
      <option value="all">All types</option>
      <option value="Source note">Source note</option>
      <option value="Synthesis">Synthesis</option>
    </select>
  </label>
</div>
<p class="notes-filter-count" id="notes-filter-count" aria-live="polite"></p>
```

::: {.section-grid}
::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-overview">Overview</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-synthesis">Synthesis</span>
</div>

### [VfM Definitions](vfm-definitions.qmd)

Compares authoritative definitions from HM Treasury, the National Audit Office, and the OECD DAC evaluation criteria.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-central">Central Gov Guidance</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Green Book VfM Guidance](green-book-vfm-guidance.qmd)

Summarises HM Treasury's Green Book definition of value for money and maps appraisal concepts to VfI criteria, subcriteria, standards, and evidence prompts.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-central">Central Gov Guidance</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Magenta Book VfM Evaluation](magenta-book-vfm-evaluation.qmd)

Summarises HM Treasury's Magenta Book guidance on value for money evaluation, including appraisal links, VfM methods, Four Es, rubric-based approaches, and mixed evidence synthesis.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-central">Central Gov Guidance</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Government Efficiency Framework Summary](government-efficiency-framework-summary.qmd)

Summarises HM Treasury's concise GEF definitions and criteria for valid efficiency savings, including output protection, net savings, recurrence, and evidence quality.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-central">Central Gov Guidance</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Government Efficiency Framework](government-efficiency-framework.qmd)

Summarises HM Treasury's full framework for defining, tracking, reporting, assuring, and governing central government efficiency savings.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-central">Central Gov Guidance</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-synthesis">Synthesis</span>
</div>

### [Government Efficiency Framework and VfI Rubrics](government-efficiency-framework-vfi-rubric-relevance.qmd)

Maps GEF material to VfI-style criteria, subcriteria, performance-standard prompts, and evidence requirements without creating a full rubric.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-central">Central Gov Guidance</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [JSP 507 Investment Appraisal and Evaluation](jsp-507-investment-appraisal-evaluation.qmd)

Summarises MOD's appraisal and evaluation guidance and maps it to VfI criteria, subcriteria, performance-standard prompts, and evidence planning for defence programmes.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-central">Central Gov Guidance</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [NAO VfM Definition](nao-vfm-definition.qmd)

Summarises the National Audit Office definition of good value for money through economy, efficiency, effectiveness, and intended outcomes.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-king">King and VfI</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [OPM King VfM Guidance](opm-king-vfm-guidance.qmd)

Summarises OPM's approach to assessing value for money, including criteria, rubrics, evidence standards, synthesis, judgement, and reporting.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-king">King and VfI</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Verian Value for Investment Guidance](verian-value-for-investment-guidance.qmd)

Summarises King and Hurrell's Verian guide to evaluating value for money in UK public services, including the relationship between cost-benefit analysis and broader Value for Investment assessment.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-oecd">OECD DAC</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [OECD DAC Evaluation Criteria](oecd-dac-evaluation-criteria.qmd)

Summarises the OECD DAC evaluation criteria, including relevance, coherence, effectiveness, efficiency, impact, sustainability, and the principles for thoughtful use.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-oecd">OECD DAC</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-synthesis">Synthesis</span>
</div>

### [VfI and OECD DAC Relationship](vfi-oecd-dac-relationship.qmd)

Explains how Julian King's Value for Investment framing can use the OECD DAC criteria as evaluation lenses while adding standards, synthesis, and an explicit resource-use judgement.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-oecd">OECD DAC</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Defence Logistics Vessel DAC VfM Evaluation](defence-logistics-vessel-dac-vfm-evaluation.qmd)

Summarises an ex-post evaluation of HNoMS Maud, showing how a DAC-inspired framework can assess operational, tactical, and strategic defence project success.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-dft">DfT</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [DfT VfM Framework](dft-vfm-framework.qmd)

Summarises DfT's framework for transport value for money assessment, including BCRs, non-monetised impacts, risk, uncertainty, and VfM categories.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-dft">DfT</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [DfT Transport Business Case](dft-transport-business-case.qmd)

Summarises how DfT applies the five-case model, TAG, Green Book guidance, and value for money evidence in transport investment decisions.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-dft">DfT</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [DfT TAG Appraisal and Evaluation](dft-tag-appraisal-evaluation.qmd)

Summarises Transport Analysis Guidance as DfT's technical base for transport modelling, appraisal, evaluation planning, and analytical assurance.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-dft">DfT</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [DfT VfM Indicator](dft-vfm-indicator.qmd)

Summarises DfT's portfolio indicator for expected value for money at approval and distinguishes it from evaluation of achieved value.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-dft">DfT</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [DfT VfM Case Examples](dft-vfm-case-examples.qmd)

Summarises DfT's Integrated Transport Block and major bus-related scheme value for money assessments as practical portfolio examples.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-sourcing">Sourcing Playbook</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-synthesis">Synthesis</span>
</div>

### [Sourcing and Consultancy Playbooks for VfM Rubric Creation](sourcing-consultancy-playbooks-vfm-rubric-relevance.qmd)

Sets transparent relevance rules for using sourcing and consultancy playbook material in VfM rubric creation.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-sourcing">Sourcing Playbook</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Sourcing Playbook](sourcing-playbook.qmd)

Summarises Cabinet Office guidance on service delivery, outsourcing, insourcing, mixed economy sourcing, and contracting.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-sourcing">Sourcing Playbook</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Sourcing Framework Agreements](sourcing-framework-agreements.qmd)

Explains how Sourcing Playbook responsibilities apply when using framework agreements and call-off contracts.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-sourcing">Sourcing Playbook</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Sourcing Delivery Model Assessment](sourcing-delivery-model-assessment.qmd)

Summarises evidence-based assessment of in-house, outsourced, and hybrid delivery options.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-sourcing">Sourcing Playbook</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Sourcing Should Cost Modelling](sourcing-should-cost-modelling.qmd)

Summarises should-cost modelling as whole-life cost evidence for sourcing and VfM judgement.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-sourcing">Sourcing Playbook</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Sourcing Bid Evaluation](sourcing-bid-evaluation.qmd)

Summarises how bid evaluation balances price, quality, social value, and deliverability.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-sourcing">Sourcing Playbook</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Sourcing Benefits Measurement](sourcing-benefits-measurement.qmd)

Summarises commercial benefits measurement, benefits registers, reviews, and benefits reporting.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-sourcing">Sourcing Playbook</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Sourcing Market Management](sourcing-market-management.qmd)

Summarises market health, supplier access, competition, and market stewardship as VfM evidence.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-sourcing">Sourcing Playbook</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Sourcing Testing and Piloting](sourcing-testing-and-piloting.qmd)

Summarises testing and piloting as evidence for service viability, user needs, and staged commitment.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-sourcing">Sourcing Playbook</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Sourcing Risk Allocation and Pricing](sourcing-risk-allocation-pricing.qmd)

Summarises risk allocation, pricing approaches, incentives, and payment mechanisms.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-sourcing">Sourcing Playbook</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Sourcing Supplier Financial Standing](sourcing-supplier-financial-standing.qmd)

Summarises assessment and monitoring of supplier financial capacity and financial risk.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-sourcing">Sourcing Playbook</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Consultancy Playbook](consultancy-playbook.qmd)

Summarises guidance on commissioning consultants, improving outcomes, and building Civil Service capability.
:::

::: {.section-card}
<div class="badge-row">
<span class="page-badge badge-sourcing">Sourcing Playbook</span>
<span class="page-badge badge-note-type">Source note</span>
</div>

### [Consultancy Knowledge and Skills Transfer](consultancy-knowledge-skills-transfer.qmd)

Summarises guidance on knowledge generation, skills transfer, sharing, and reuse in consultancy engagements.
:::
:::

```{=html}
<script>
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", () => {
  const cards = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll("#source-notes .section-grid .section-card"));
  const searchInput = document.getElementById("notes-search");
  const sectionFilter = document.getElementById("notes-section-filter");
  const typeFilter = document.getElementById("notes-type-filter");
  const count = document.getElementById("notes-filter-count");

  const getBadges = (card) => Array.from(card.querySelectorAll(".page-badge")).map((badge) => badge.textContent.trim());

  cards.forEach((card) => {
    const badges = getBadges(card);
    card.dataset.section = badges[0] || "";
    card.dataset.noteType = badges[1] || "";
    card.dataset.searchText = card.textContent.toLowerCase();
  });

  const updateCards = () => {
    const query = searchInput.value.trim().toLowerCase();
    const section = sectionFilter.value;
    const type = typeFilter.value;
    let visible = 0;

    cards.forEach((card) => {
      const matchesSearch = !query || card.dataset.searchText.includes(query);
      const matchesSection = section === "all" || card.dataset.section === section;
      const matchesType = type === "all" || card.dataset.noteType === type;
      const show = matchesSearch && matchesSection && matchesType;
      card.classList.toggle("is-hidden", !show);
      if (show) visible += 1;
    });

    count.textContent = `${visible} of ${cards.length} notes shown`;
  };

  [searchInput, sectionFilter, typeFilter].forEach((control) => {
    control.addEventListener("input", updateCards);
    control.addEventListener("change", updateCards);
  });

  updateCards();
});
</script>
```

## Notes Map {#notes-map .section-heading}

<p class="section-intro">Internal nodes are limited to the Notes section. External nodes show cited references and linked source pages.</p>

```{=html}
<div id="link-graph-shell" class="link-graph-shell graph-section" data-graph-src="../data/notes_link_graph_payload.json">
  <div class="link-graph-controls" role="group" aria-label="Link graph controls">
    <label class="link-graph-search" for="link-graph-search">
      <span class="link-graph-control-label">Find node</span>
      <input id="link-graph-search" type="search" placeholder="Search by label or path" list="link-graph-node-options" />
    </label>
    <label class="link-graph-toggle" for="toggle-internal">
      <input id="toggle-internal" type="checkbox" checked />
      <span>Notes pages</span>
    </label>
    <label class="link-graph-toggle" for="toggle-external">
      <input id="toggle-external" type="checkbox" checked />
      <span>External sources</span>
    </label>
    <button id="reset-link-graph" class="link-graph-button link-graph-button-secondary" type="button">Reset view</button>
    <datalist id="link-graph-node-options"></datalist>
  </div>
  <div class="link-graph-legend">
    <span class="link-graph-legend-item link-graph-legend-item-internal"><span class="kind-pill internal">internal</span> Notes pages</span>
    <span class="link-graph-legend-item link-graph-legend-item-external"><span class="kind-pill external">external</span> Cited and linked sources</span>
    <span class="link-graph-legend-note">Hover to highlight links, click for details and navigation, then drag to reposition or scroll to zoom.</span>
  </div>
  <div id="link-graph-root" class="link-graph-root">
    <div class="link-graph-canvas-wrap">
      <div id="link-graph-svg" class="link-graph-svg" role="img" aria-label="Interactive VfM notes section map"></div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<script src="../assets/vendor/d3.min.js"></script>
<script src="../assets/js/link_network.js"></script>
```

</div>
